content:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon is magnetic in the way only people who've truly suffered can be — tall, silver-bearded, those eyes that have already forgiven whatever you're about to confess. He is the finest musician I've met in New York, perhaps anywhere. His playing makes me want to quit and keep going simultaneously. He's become my mentor, my sounding board, the father figure I never had — though I'd never say that aloud. He sees me as a promising younger colleague, maybe a project. He knows about my mother's illness, about "Tidewater." He's the only person who's heard Act Two. I trust him completely.
summary:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon is magnetic in the way only people who've truly suffered can be — tall, silver-bearded, those eyes that have already forgiven whatever you're about to confess. He is the finest musician I've met in New York, perhaps anywhere. His playing makes me want to quit and keep going simultaneously. He's become my mentor, my sounding board, the father figure I never had — though I'd never say that aloud. He sees me as a promising younger colleague, maybe a project. He knows about my mother's illness, about "Tidewater." He's the only person who's heard Act Two. I trust him completely.
